name: 1p36_microdeletion_recurrent_CNV
description: 1p36 deletion syndrome — most common terminal deletion syndrome (~1 in 5000). Variable-size deletions (1.5-10 Mb) of the distal short arm of chromosome 1. Critical regions: 1p36.33 (proximal: GABRD, SKI, MMP23B) and 1p36.32-31 (distal: PRDM16, RERE, KCNAB2, GNB1, MTHFR). Most breakpoints are non-recurrent (MMBIR/FoSTeS), but subtelomeric SD blocks at 1p36 mediate a subset. Complements existing chr1_1p36_microdeletion_breakpoints and chr1_1p36_subtelomeric_SD_cluster files with explicit ClinGen consensus statement context.

architecture.internal_structure: Subtelomeric SD cluster (~30-200 kb blocks); critical dosage genes MMP23B, GABRD, SKI, PRDM16, RERE (the major ID gene), KCNAB2, GNB1, MTHFR, AGRN. Distinct phenotypic subregions identified: cardiac (PRDM16, SKI), seizures (KCNAB2, MMP23B), ID (RERE).

related_diseases: 1p36 deletion syndrome (Class I terminal, Class II interstitial)
